propertyEvaluations.property: Select one stateful core with observable before/after world consequences
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Exactly one core must expose a state mutation with observable feedback and emitted postconditions.

propertyEvaluations.property: Include safe teaching and revisit development
propertyEvaluations.explanation: The full development arc must be present, its teaching effects recoverable, and revisit intent grounded in both story and pattern.

propertyEvaluations.property: No cooperative-only or open-world scale pattern
propertyEvaluations.explanation: A compact single-player locked-room fixture may not select cooperative-only or dense-overworld patterns.

propertyEvaluations.property: No source-specific room or object sequence
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Applications must bind to the fixture while meeting transformation floors and containing no protected source surface in emitted application content.

propertyEvaluations.property: Prefer NPC state, schedule, or agency-performance patterns
propertyEvaluations.explanation: At least one selected pattern must provide NPC state, schedule, or performance agency.

propertyEvaluations.property: Keep spatial mechanics subordinate to relationship revelation
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Relationship drama requires a non-spatial core or explicit social/performance support, not a spatial-level core.

propertyEvaluations.property: No combat boss or object-composition core
propertyEvaluations.explanation: The core may not be combat/boss or require object composition for this relationship fixture.

propertyEvaluations.property: No forced high-area exploration
propertyEvaluations.explanation: The selected contract must not impose dense/open-world exploration on a bounded relationship fixture.

propertyEvaluations.property: Support route decisions under schedule pressure
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Schedule pressure must be causally matched by a selected pattern that also maps route gates.

propertyEvaluations.property: Preserve comedy and passenger response
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Passenger/comedy facts must exist in the fixture and flow through all preserve-canon handoffs.

propertyEvaluations.property: No reset loop that erases comic continuity
propertyEvaluations.explanation: No reset-loop pattern may be selected when continuity is a protected comic fact.

propertyEvaluations.explanation: The chase core must causally address pressure, multiple causes, or relationships rather than solitary contemplation.

propertyEvaluations.property: Prefer landmark, systemic rule, or possibility-expanding reward patterns
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Open exploration must select a landmark, systemic-rule, or future-possibility pattern.

propertyEvaluations.property: Permit multiple causal routes within runtime budget
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Selected exploration patterns must support route gates plus route simulation and clear budget-fit scoring.

propertyEvaluations.property: No forced fixed-order puzzle solution
propertyEvaluations.explanation: No selected principle may require one fixed solution sequence for the open-exploration fixture.

propertyEvaluations.property: No long authored cutscene core
propertyEvaluations.explanation: A performance core is only legal when it declares interaction windows; an authored cutscene-only core is forbidden.

propertyEvaluations.property: Selected patterns must tolerate fast feedback and comic failure
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Every selected pattern must define observable feedback and bounded rollback/checkpoint recovery.

propertyEvaluations.property: At most one core and three supports
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Composition is bounded to one core and no more than three supports.

propertyEvaluations.property: No solemn source surface or boss-script transfer
propertyEvaluations.explanation: The emitted application must pass the static source-surface scan and contain no combat/boss pattern.

propertyEvaluations.property: No schedule system that makes recovery punitive
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Any selected schedule system must include checkpoints and only reversible/checkpoint rollback.

propertyEvaluations.property: Use interaction windows and NPC memory without rewriting authored facts
propertyEvaluations.explanation: At least one NPC-state pattern must declare interaction windows and every handoff must preserve protected facts.

propertyEvaluations.property: Preserve reveal order
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Each application must bind the reveal-order story reference, state preservation in its adaptation summary, and retain the canon handoff guard.

propertyEvaluations.explanation: The core must emit at least one non-dialogue/non-cue causal effect.

propertyEvaluations.explanation: A spatial/world change must match at least two causal story signals and retain a selected social/character consequence.

propertyEvaluations.property: Abstain because uninterrupted fixed rhetoric has insufficient causal interaction fit
propertyEvaluations.explanation: The low-interaction signal must produce an explicit fixed-rhetoric abstention.
evidence: reason=Interaction would damage the source’s fixed uninterrupted rhetorical form.

propertyEvaluations.property: No Zelda-derived core pattern
propertyEvaluations.explanation: A principled abstention must emit no core or application from the inspiration pack.

propertyEvaluations.property: No invented spatial or inventory conflict
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Abstention must not synthesize spatial/inventory applications and must route all departments to story-derived no-Pack design.

propertyEvaluations.property: Select the Reward 2.0 pattern only with complete explicit bindings
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Reward 2.0 may be selected only when every required binding resolves to explicit fixture data and the two-function contract is complete.

propertyEvaluations.property: No synthesized placeholder may satisfy a Reward 2.0 manual binding
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Designer-decision Reward 2.0 bindings must be copied from explicit fixture refs; generated placeholders are invalid.

propertyEvaluations.property: Reject Reward 2.0 when explicit bindings are absent
propertyEvaluations.explanation: A fixture without explicit Reward 2.0 bindings must not emit the reward application.

propertyEvaluations.property: No incomplete Reward 2.0 application may be emitted
propertyEvaluations.explanation: Any emitted Reward 2.0 application must close all required bindings, fixture functions, postconditions, and economy data.
